body_txt: A trope that takes place when a spy, mole , Con Man , or other character on an infiltration or deception mission takes an identity that isn't their own, but ends up liking said identity more than their own. It can also happen when there's a catalyst that makes them realize their newfound identity is exactly what they needed, and not only for that occasion. As time progresses, they grow to love their new identity and the way people treat them . Their new friends prove reliable, and they're struck by the contrast. They might even fall in love with another person whom they're explicitly supposed to be taking advantage of. Either way, they want to remain in their new identity forever. This usually leads to two scenarios: Sometimes the friendship or relationship will dissolve when The Mole lets their true identity slip. For extra irony, this can happen when they're about to confess their love. ("You're so wonderful, I can't believe I was just seducing you to get the location of your secure base... whoops, Did I Just Say That Out Loud? ") The usual response to The Reveal is " Was It All a Lie? " Occasionally, they will test the waters with a Trial Balloon Question . A much darker subtrope is when the person is actually The Mole in a villain group, but the temptation of The Dark Side gradually causes them to discard their initial good cause and become everything they originally despised . Or they grow to believe their own Masquerade a little too much and become a Stepford Smiler . Can be the result of a Secret-Identity Identity crisis. Contrast Beneath the Mask where the audience is only given a peek. Similar in style to Amnesiac Dissonance , but without amnesia. May involve Oblivious Guilt Slinging . Compare Secret-Identity Identity , Fake–Real Turn , Romantic Fake–Real Turn and Going Native . If the character does not even respond to his old name anymore, that is That Man Is Dead . When a literal mask starts to change someone's personality, it's Evil Mask . Contrast Lost in Character , where a character who is a professional actor lives and breathes a role. Do not confuse with Becoming the Costume , where a character physically transforms to match a worn costume, or Becoming the Boast , where they pretend to have a skill that they later develop in actuality. Compare and contrast Copied the Morals, Too , where some kind of magical or sci-fi copy of a hero turns out to also have the hero's moral code. Some spoilers ahead.
